$note: Captured snapshot of the COMPARED forecast/intelligence product for the azimuth benchmark (synthesis/benchmark.py). Quoted by product + capture date; NOT surfaced as an azimuth channel and NOT an L1 source. Refresh with pull_benchmark_foils.py.

foil.title: Inflation and rates pressure from Venezuela infrastructure fragility state
foil.scenario_short: Venezuela infrastructure fragility state is feeding inflation and rates pressure through energy supply shock, with 50% pressure and 59% state confirmation. For now, the base case stays near 57% over the 30d. Actors...
foil.perspective_strategic: Venezuela infrastructure fragility state is feeding inflation and rates pressure through energy supply shock, with 50% pressure and 59% state confirmation is setting the strategic baseline, and the current 55% probability implies a live but not settled risk path.
foil.perspective_contrarian: No directly matched headlines are currently attached to Venezuela. If that restraint persists, the forecast can move below the current 55% baseline. Actors prioritize containment and the system drifts toward stabilization unless new hard signals emerge.
foil.actor_lens: Commodity traders: Price whether stress in Venezuela becomes durable over the 30d. Rebalance positions if the probability path moves away from 55%.
foil.top_signal: Venezuela infrastructure fragility state is feeding inflation and rates pressure through energy supply shock, with 50% pressure and 59% state confirmation

foil.title: Inflation and rates pressure from Black Sea maritime disruption state
foil.scenario_short: Inflation and rates pressure in the Black Sea region is probable at 60%, driven by shipping cost shock with 50% pressure and 72% state confirmation, and reinforced by 7 urgent critical signals.
foil.perspective_strategic: The 60% probability of inflation and rates pressure, driven by shipping cost shock (50% pressure, 72% state confirmation), indicates a significant but not overwhelming risk to global markets from the Black Sea region.
foil.perspective_contrarian: Despite 7 urgent critical signals reinforcing pressure, the current 75% confidence level suggests that actors are not yet fully convinced of an escalating scenario, leaving room for a reversal if containment efforts prove effective.
foil.actor_lens: Commodity traders: Price whether stress in Black Sea becomes durable over the 30d. Rebalance positions if the probability path moves away from 60%.
foil.top_signal: Black Sea maritime disruption state is feeding inflation and rates pressure through shipping cost shock, with 50% pressure and 72% state confirmation

seismic-infrastructure.absence_reason: No product publishes a seismic forecast — earthquakes are not predictable, so there is no forecast/intel alternative to azimuth's observed USGS record. This is the honest inverse: for an observed natural-hazard fact, the sourced record IS the only trustworthy source.
